skill.name: creating-skills
skill.description: Guides users through creating reusable AI skill definitions with naming rules, templates, validation steps, and documentation practices.
skill.summary: Creates consistent skill documentation with templates, naming guidance, and validation checklists.
skill.author: BPSAI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many Ruby backtick and weak cryptography patterns, but the reviewed file is Markdown documentation. The suspicious hits are code fences, inline examples, field names, and command snippets for skill validation, not executable code or obfuscated behavior. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, network access, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: Documented Local Command Examples
low_findings.description: The skill includes command examples for listing, validating, and creating skill files. These commands are visible documentation for a skill-authoring workflow and do not execute automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands appear inside Markdown instructions and code fences. They are local validation and directory creation examples with no network, secret access, or automatic execution path.

low_findings.title: Static Pattern False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance hits map to Markdown fences, inline code formatting, field names, examples, and headings. No executable Ruby, cryptographic logic, or system reconnaissance behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review shows natural-language documentation and Markdown syntax rather than runnable code. The suspicious tokens are examples such as field names, filenames, and formatting markers.

content.value_statement: Teams often repeat workflows without a clear way to capture the process. This skill provides a concise structure for writing, validating, and maintaining reusable AI skill definitions.
actual_capabilities: Defines when a repeated workflow should become a skill.
Provides a SKILL.md template with required frontmatter fields.
Explains naming conventions for lowercase, hyphenated, gerund skill names.
Lists validation checks for descriptions, file length, and frontmatter.
Shows local CLI commands for listing and validating skills.
Identifies common skill authoring errors and direct fixes.
limitations: Does not create files automatically unless the user runs the documented commands.
Does not validate marketplace policy beyond the listed formatting checks.
Does not include advanced examples for multi-file skills or scripts.
Does not provide tool-specific packaging steps outside the documented workflow.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Workflows
use_cases.description: Convert repeated team procedures into consistent skill documents with clear triggers and steps.

use_cases.title: Create a First Skill
use_cases.description: Use the template and checklist to draft a compliant SKILL.md file.

use_cases.title: Review Existing Skills
use_cases.description: Check naming, description style, frontmatter, and file length before sharing a skill.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner author creating a first skill.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a SKILL.md for a repeated workflow. Ask for the workflow goal, trigger conditions, and main steps.

prompt_templates.title: Check Skill Naming
prompt_templates.scenario: Author wants to validate a proposed skill name.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this skill name and description against the naming and frontmatter rules. Suggest corrected text if needed.

prompt_templates.title: Turn Notes Into a Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert these process notes into a focused skill document with a clear trigger, steps, commands, and examples.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Skill Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this SKILL.md against the validation checklist. Report required fixes, optional improvements, and any unclear workflow assumptions.

output_examples.input: I need a skill for reviewing release notes before publishing.
output_examples.output: A concise skill outline with a gerund name, a third-person description, trigger conditions, review steps, and validation reminders.

output_examples.input: Check whether my skill follows the format rules.
output: Frontmatter includes only name and description.
Name uses lowercase hyphenated gerund form.
Description is short, third person, and under the limit.

best_practices: Create skills only for repeated workflows with clear triggers.
Keep SKILL.md focused and move supporting detail into reference documents.
Validate names, descriptions, and frontmatter before sharing a skill.
anti_patterns: Do not document generic knowledge that the model already knows.
Do not add unsupported metadata fields to skill frontmatter.
Do not use second-person descriptions or names that differ from the directory.

faq.question: What problem does this skill solve?
faq.answer: It helps authors turn repeated workflows into consistent skill documents that are easier to validate and reuse.

faq.question: Which files does the skill describe?
faq.answer: It focuses on SKILL.md and optional supporting reference or script folders.

faq.question: Does it run commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It documents local command examples that a user may choose to run.

faq.question: What naming style does it recommend?
faq.answer: It recommends lowercase, hyphenated, gerund names that match the skill directory.

faq.question: Can it help fix validation errors?
faq.answer: Yes. It lists common validation errors and the expected correction for each one.

faq.question: Is it suitable for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The marketplace metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
